fresh_facts.claim: The US deployed additional military assets to the Middle East including 1,000 additional troops and at least 2,000 paratroopers from the 82nd Airborne Division, along with two Marine combat groups totaling 5,000-6,000 troops, following collapsed ceasefire talks in Islamabad.
President Trump announced a sweeping naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz on April 12, 2026, directing the US Navy to interdict ships and clear naval mines, effective immediately.
IRGC forces claimed to lock missiles and drones on a US destroyer in the Strait of Hormuz, with released footage of radio warnings dated around April 12, 2026.
US CENTCOM denied any confrontation occurred between US and IRGC forces in the Strait of Hormuz.
Israel continued strikes against Hezbollah in Lebanon, hitting over 200 targets in the south to support ground operations up to the Latani River.
No Israeli military operations or threats directly against Iran were reported in the past 24 hours as of April 12, 2026.
Netanyahu stated on April 11 that Israeli operations have impacted Iran's nuclear and missile capabilities and weakened Iran-aligned groups.
Quds Force commander Esmail Qa'ani declared the Axis of Resistance remains strong following the collapse of ceasefire talks.
Iran-backed Hezbollah leader Sheikh Naim Qassem vowed continued retaliation despite pressure, signaling full mobilization of proxies.
Tehran accused the US of unlawful demands and signaled readiness to respond with force to the US blockade.
US Vice President JD Vance led negotiations in Islamabad on April 12, 2026, which ended without agreement on Iran's nuclear program.
Prior US-Israel attacks on Iran began February 28, 2026, with a two-week attack pause that expired as of April 12, 2026.
A two-week ceasefire mediated by Pakistan is holding tenuously after six weeks of fighting, with mutual accusations of violations.
President Masoud Pezeshkian faces fierce hardline backlash for signaling conditional willingness to end the war.
The IRGC pressured President Pezeshkian to appoint Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr as Supreme National Security Council secretary.
Serious disagreements exist between President Pezeshkian and IRGC chief Ahmad Vahidi over war management and economic fallout.
The IRGC has assumed key governmental functions, pushing President Pezeshkian into a complete political deadlock.
Hardliners branded ex-foreign minister Mohammad Javad Zarif a traitor and called for arresting him and ex-president Hassan Rouhani.
Over 700 arrests were made in Tehran, Kurdish, and Baluchi areas following the ceasefire in late June 2026, with IRGC and Basij raids and checkpoints.
Fatal shootings occurred on July 2, 2026, in Hamedan and Khomein during security crackdowns, according to Fars News.
Iran's Parliament proposed a mandatory death penalty for espionage to suppress dissent.
The war has worsened Iran's unstable economy, with shocks from conflict and sanctions intensifying economic strain.
Trump postponed strikes on Iran's power grid following what he characterized as constructive talks, though Tehran denied this characterization.
State media frames protests as enemy warfare and warns against revealing officials' locations.
Amnesty International criticized arrests as due process violations amid a rising repression trend in Iran.
A far-right Israeli Knesset member mocked Trump's threats toward Iran, urging him to take military action rather than make threats.

regime_fall.current_status: Following collapsed Islamabad peace talks on April 12, 2026, the Trump administration has implemented a comprehensive naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz with massive military deployments (7,000-8,000 troops plus warplanes), while Iran appointed hardliner Zolghadr as security chief and signals military readiness despite severe economic strain and internal political fracturing between President Pezeshkian and the IRGC.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Trump announced a sweeping naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz on April 12, 2026, with orders to interdict ships and clear naval mines, coupled with deployment of 1,000 additional troops, 2,000 82nd Airborne paratroopers, and two Marine combat groups totaling 5,000-6,000 troops to the Middle East.
